summary:
The brain remains notably more underdeveloped than other organs like eyes and ears due to ongoing neural network formation and specialization required during infancy and early childhood. This critical period necessitates extensive support outside utero for full cognitive development potential. Summarizing Agent1's reasoning into two concise sentences while focusing only on clinically significant aspects related directly addressing which major anatomical structure stands out regarding developmental immaturity upon birth reveals a clear emphasis on cerebral significance given prolonged dependence even past initial extrusion event marking end of pregnancy phase; specifically noting extended necessity non-physiological assistance integral achieving maximal intellectual/behavioral outcomes aligns closely with core query intent pinpointing primary area lagging behind others physiologically speaking right away newborn stage emphasizing unique nature requiring special care attention fostering proper growth trajectory essential realizing innate capacity optimally expressed later life stages.
